historicalEvidence.failureClass: identical binding-scope content produced different verdicts after pull_request context disappeared

rules.pull_request-only: merge-blind unless an explicit per-path exception exists
rules.push/schedule/workflow_dispatch-only: intentional-by-design post-merge, scheduled, or operator surface

rows.reason: The old resolver returned a lenient verdict when PR-only environment existed and capture objects were unavailable.
rows.remediation: Use one fail-closed capture-to-checked-out-HEAD content comparison. PR head discovery remains diagnostics only and cannot change pass/fail.

rows.reason: captureCommitSha was interpreted as current commit identity in fallback paths.
rows.remediation: Declare bindingMode=scoped-tree-content-v1; the SHA identifies the source snapshot whose guarded content is compared to checked-out HEAD.

rows.reason: The explicit producer records artifact provenance and binding mode; it does not decide PR/main pass or fail.
PR context used pull_request.base.sha while push context could compare HEAD to origin/main=HEAD; short prerequisite commit IDs coupled the verdict to history identity.
rows.remediation: Resolve a non-self comparison base and verify #709/#711 semantic surfaces at that base and HEAD.

rows.reason: The push fallback could pass origin/main=HEAD to the protected-runtime diff and silently evaluate an empty range.
rows.remediation: Delegate base selection to the non-self resolver and fail closed when no prior boundary exists.

rows.reason: The first resolvable candidate was accepted even when its merge-base was HEAD.
rows.remediation: Reject self merge-bases and fall back to HEAD^1, preserving a pre-change boundary in PR and push contexts.

rows.reason: Every candidate is reduced to a merge-base, merge-base==HEAD is rejected, and HEAD^1 is the final context-neutral fallback.
The core evaluates current-tree lane coverage, topology, and the fail-closed RPC content validator; IDs are provenance only.
The mandatory wrapper runs the dynamic audit and PR/push run-twice fixture before the existing topology wrapper/core.
